steps.title: Apply Tremblay's test
steps.content: Tremblay questions the idea that the tusk has an important purpose because many narwhals do not have one. If the tusk were important for avoiding dangerous freezing water, it would be expected to appear more widely in the population.

steps.title: Remove unrelated outcomes
steps.content: A shifts to habitat, which the text never predicts. B discusses other marine animals, not narwhals. D brings in shyness, which is unrelated to the tusk's proposed function. The answer is C.
